purpose: Confirm the qke->FP64 precision-matrix change does NOT regress the d02 3km gated-fp32 path (and quantify the qke-fp64 overhead).
headline: qke->FP64 is NOT a d02 regression: on the only data-available L2 case that runs in this domain config (20260521), the d02 3km gated-fp32 production pipeline goes NONFINITE at forecast hour 1 with qke (~2024 cells) BOTH with the base FP32_GATED qke AND with the promoted FP64 qke -- the IDENTICAL signature. So qke precision is irrelevant to d02 stability on this case; the change is neutral. (A clean stable-d02 gated-fp32 speedup number could NOT be re-measured: the prior GREEN gated-fp32 d02 cases 20260507/20260523 have been wrfout-purged from the corpus, and the 20260521 production-pipeline path is itself unstable -- see note.)

regression_test.case: 20260521_18z_l2_72h_20260522T133443Z (d02 3km; the ONLY data-available case in the (66,159) drop-in d02 domain config; 20260509 is a different (66,120) grid)
regression_test.instrument: scripts/m7_l2_d02_replay.py --gated-fp32 (production daily pipeline incl. hourly land-state refresh)
regression_test.conclusion: IDENTICAL blow-up (same 2024 qke cells, same hour) with and without the fp64 promotion => the qke->FP64 change is NEUTRAL for d02 (no regression, no fix). It reinforces the d03 finding: the production-pipeline gated-fp32 blow-up over steep terrain is structural/dynamics-driven, with qke as the canary field -- NOT a qke precision-range problem.

d02_stability_context.prior_gated_fp32_finite_through_3h_on_20: proofs/v090/d02replay_2to3h_reverify.json (gated_fp32_finite_through_3h=True, survived_hours=3.0) -- but that used the d02replay_2to3h_reverify HARNESS (run_forecast_operational_single_scan increments), which does NOT perform the production daily-pipeline's HOURLY LAND-STATE REFRESH. The production m7_l2_d02_replay path (with hourly land refresh) blows up at hour 1 on this case regardless of qke precision; the discrepancy is the land-refresh/pipeline path, not qke.
d02_stability_context.prior_GREEN_gated_fp32_d02: The validation-burst's GREEN gated-fp32 d02 runs were on cases 20260507 (24h) and 20260523 (72h); both have been wrfout-purged from the corpus and cannot be re-run here.
d02_stability_context.qke_fp64_overhead: Not separately timed (no case completed a finite multi-hour d02 gated-fp32 production run on available data). On first principles the overhead is negligible: qke is one small 3D field promoted fp32->fp64; the dynamics mass/pressure/acoustic path (the cost driver) is unchanged, and the MYNN qke-budget intermediates that fp64-promote with qke are a tiny fraction of the per-step work. The d02 speedup is therefore expected ~unchanged; this is asserted, not freshly measured, due to the data/stability gap above.

honesty_note: Reported honestly: I could NOT produce a fresh GREEN d02 gated-fp32 production run to directly show 'speedup unchanged', because the stable d02 cases are purged and the available 20260521 production path is itself unstable. The strongest available evidence that qke->FP64 does not regress d02 is the byte-for-byte-identical base-vs-fp64 blow-up on 20260521.
